reasoning:
The FRED series for the Cleveland Fed's 10-Year Real Interest Rate registered at 1.566 as of August 2025. This reading sits securely at the lower end of the series' 12-month range (which averaged approximately 1.75 and only reached lower in October 2024 at 1.48). Mean reversion tendencies and local volatility suggest a high likelihood of a bounce in the immediate future; an automated linear and seasonal estimate gives roughly a 67% chance of an increase relative to this trough. 

Therefore, for the near-term resolution date (Sept 2025), I assign a probability of 0.65, capturing the expectation of a reversion to the mean. Consistent with the instruction to increase uncertainty proportionally with the forecast horizon, the longer-term estimates for Nov 2025 and Jan 2026 are shrunk progressively toward 0.5, adjusting for the accumulating unforeseeable macroeconomic impacts over longer durations.
